verdict_reasoning: BAX is canonically a cytosolic/mitochondrial-outer-membrane pro-apoptotic effector that oligomerizes on the MOM to permeabilize it. During apoptosis and immunogenic cell death, BAX (and family members) have documented externalization/exposure on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane of dying cells, and tumor cells can display ecto-BAX. This cell-death-induced surface exposure qualifies as contextual, not no.
